mission.title: Install and Configure Charmed Kubernetes
mission.description: Charmed Kubernetes is a distribution of Kubernetes that is designed for easy deployment and management using Juju. It is ideal for users looking for a robust Kubernetes solution that integrates well with Ubuntu and offers modern metrics and monitoring capabilities. Key trade-offs include the need for Juju and the reliance on Ubuntu as the underlying OS.

steps.title: Install Juju
steps.description:
Juju is the tool used to deploy and manage Charmed Kubernetes. This step installs Juju on your system.

```bash

sudo snap install juju --classic

```

> Installs the latest stable version of Juju.

steps.title: Bootstrap Juju Controller
steps.description:
This command initializes a Juju controller, which is necessary for managing your Kubernetes deployment.

```bash

juju bootstrap localhost

```

> Bootstraps a Juju controller on your local machine.

steps.title: Deploy Charmed Kubernetes
steps.description:
This step deploys the Charmed Kubernetes bundle using Juju.

```bash

juju deploy charmed-kubernetes

```

> Deploys the latest version of Charmed Kubernetes.

steps.title: Verify Deployment
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the deployed services to ensure everything is running correctly.

```bash

juju status

```

> Displays the status of all deployed services.

steps.title: Configure Kubernetes Access
steps.description:
This step sets up your kubeconfig file to access the Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

juju config kubernetes-control-plane kubeconfig

```

> Fetches the kubeconfig for accessing the cluster.

uninstall.title: Remove Charmed Kubernetes
uninstall.description:
This command will remove the Charmed Kubernetes deployment and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

juju destroy-controller localhost --destroy-all-models

```

> Destroys the Juju controller and all models.

uninstall.title: Cleanup Orphaned Resources
uninstall.description:
This step ensures that any orphaned resources such as load balancers or persistent volumes are removed.

```bash

juju remove-application charmed-kubernetes

```

> Removes the application from Juju.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Charmed Kubernetes
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your current configuration and data. This step will upgrade your Charmed Kubernetes deployment.

```bash

juju upgrade-charm charmed-kubernetes

```

> Upgrades the Charmed Kubernetes charm to the latest version.

upgrade.title: Verify Upgrade
upgrade.description:
After the upgrade, check the status of the services to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

juju status

```

> Displays the status of all deployed services after the upgrade.

upgrade.title: Rollback if Necessary
upgrade.description:
If the upgrade fails, you can rollback to the previous version.

```bash

juju rollback charmed-kubernetes

```

> Rolls back the Charmed Kubernetes application to the previous version.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'juju bootstrap failed'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the local environment does not meet the requirements for Juju.

**Fix:**
Ensure that your system meets the minimum requirements for Juju and try bootstrapping again.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'charmed-kubernetes not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Charmed Kubernetes bundle has not been deployed.

**Fix:**
Run 'juju deploy charmed-kubernetes' to deploy the bundle.

troubleshooting.title: Kubernetes services are not running
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may happen if the Juju deployment failed or if there are resource constraints.

**Fix:**
Check the Juju status with 'juju status' and troubleshoot any failed units.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubeconfig not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the kubeconfig file has not been generated or configured.

**Fix:**
Run 'juju config kubernetes-control-plane kubeconfig' to fetch the kubeconfig.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes 1.31, improved metrics collection, and enhanced monitoring capabilities.
versionNotes.deprecations: No specific deprecations noted in this version.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps are required when upgrading from prior versions.

resolution.summary: You should see output from 'juju status' indicating that all services are active and running, confirming a successful deployment.

prerequisites.description: Ensure your system meets the resource requirements and that Juju is installed.
